content:
The deploy correlation was real. The timestamp alignment was fiction.

6 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

2:31 PM UTC. Search API p99 latency 4.2s. Baseline 180ms. SEV2.
On-call pulled logs. Found error spike in search-indexer at 14:28. Deploy annotation on catalog-api at 14:29. Obvious story: catalog deploy broke search.
Rolled back catalog-api at 3:02 PM. Latency unchanged. 31 minutes wasted.
3:23 PM. Someone asked: “what timezone is the indexer logging in?”
Indexer: America/Los_Angeles (PST). Catalog deploy annotation: UTC. Application logs on search-api: Europe/Berlin (CET) because German contractor shipped the logging config in 2019.
The 14:28 indexer spike was 22:28 UTC the previous evening. Unrelated cron. Catalog deploy at 14:29 UTC was innocent.
Actual cause: search-api connection pool exhaustion from a 2:14 PM UTC traffic spike. Visible only when logs aligned.
52 minutes of wrong rollback. Right service. Wrong clock.
How three timezones hide in plain sight
# search-indexer pod — Los Angeles
kubectl logs search-indexer-7f8a | head -1
# 2026-03-12 14:28:01 PST ERROR reindex failed shard=7# catalog-api deploy — Grafana annotation
# 2026-03-12 14:29:00 UTC — deploy v3.2.1# search-api app log — Berlin
kubectl logs search-api-9c2d | head -1
# 2026-03-12 15:31:00 CET WARN pool exhausted waiting=847Three timestamps. Three zones. One human correlating by eye.
14:28 before 14:29 looks like causation. It was eight hours apart in reality.
The correlation trap
Brains pattern-match. Logs reward confidence.
What we thought:
→ Indexer error at T-1 minute → Catalog deploy at T → Rollback fixes it (it didn’t)
What was true:
→ Pool exhaustion at 14:14 UTC on search-api → Indexer errors 22:28 PST = stale red herring from nightly job → Catalog deploy coincidental
# After normalization to UTC
jq -r '.timestamp + " " + .message' search-api.json | grep "pool exhausted"
# 2026-03-12T14:14:33Z WARN pool exhausted waiting=847# Indexer "spike" in UTC
# 2026-03-13T06:28:01Z ERROR reindex failed # previous night job, not incidentPool fix: bump max connections + kill idle holders. 4 minutes once we stopped chasing catalog.
UTC everywhere — the rule nobody enforced until Tuesday
Policy now:
→ All application logs emit ISO-8601 with Z suffix → All infrastructure timestamps UTC in Grafana → Deploy annotations UTC only → Loki/ELK ingest normalizes to UTC at collection → TZ=UTC in all Kubernetes pod specs for new services
Legacy exception process: service owner documents offset in runbook header. No undocumented zones.
# logging config — what we mandate now
logging:
format: json
timestamp: iso8601_utc # %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%fZGerman contractor’s CET config was “works on my machine” from 2019. Cost 52 minutes in 2026.
The five-minute timeline reconstruction ritual
Before blaming a deploy correlation:

• Convert all sources to UTC (script, not mental math)

• List events in single sorted timeline

• Mark confidence: verified vs coincidental proximity

• Identify earliest customer-impacting event

• Only then pick rollback candidate
# inc-timeline.sh — ugly but saved us
#!/bin/bash
# Usage: inc-timeline.sh loki-query deploy-id
logcli query "$1" --timezone=UTC -o raw | sort
curl -s grafana/api/annotations/$2 | jq -r '.time + " deploy " + .text'52 minutes → 8 minutes with the script on replay.
→ 30 Production Incidents That Cost $10K+ — wrong-service rollbacks from bad correlation show up repeatedly. Timestamp skew is a boring root cause with an expensive symptom.
Grafana deploy annotations vs application logs
Deploy annotations: reliable UTC. Application logs: zoo.
Fix stack:
→ OpenTelemetry collector enforces UTC at export → Loki timestamp_ns from normalized field, not raw line → Dashboard variable: "display timezone" for humans, storage always UTC
On-call dashboards default UTC. Local display is cosmetic. Correlation uses storage.
Tuesday postmortem action: audit 47 services for logging timezone. 11 had non-UTC. 3 weeks to remediate. Cheaper than another 52-minute rollback to the wrong service.
What rollback cost besides time
catalog-api v3.2.1 rollback:
→ Reverted legitimate bug fix shipped same deploy → Re-shipped Thursday with extra change window → 2 hours engineer time re-validating → Customer impact: zero improvement during wrong rollback window
Right fix on search-api pool: config change, no deploy. Could have been 14:20 UTC resolution. Landed 15:23 UTC.
40 minutes of elevated latency customers actually felt — after wrong path exhausted.
Honest opinion after three months of UTC enforcement
Trend piece: I was skeptical. “Developers think in local time.” True. Incidents think in UTC. Local time is for standup. UTC is for causality.
Three months in:
→ Deploy correlation false positives down ~70% (measured in postmortem tags) → Mean time to correct service identification down 18 minutes median → One remaining offender: vendor SaaS logs in US/Pacific with no TZ in string — documented, avoided in correlation
Nobody tells you the logging config from 2019 kills search on a Tuesday. They tell you to check the deploy graph. Check the clocks first.
Vendor logs — the fourth timezone
After Tuesday we found a fourth source: payment webhook vendor logs in US/Pacific with no offset in the string.
# vendor export — ambiguous
2026-03-12 14:14:05 ERROR webhook delivery failed
# is that Pacific? UTC? nobody knows without opening a ticketRule: vendor logs excluded from automated correlation until TZ confirmed. Manual only.
Saved us once in April — vendor “spike” looked 1 minute before our deploy. Was 9 hours after. Same class of trap.
The inc-timeline.sh output that ended the debate
Sorted UTC timeline at 3:23 PM:
2026-03-12T14:14:33Z search-api pool exhausted
2026-03-12T14:29:00Z catalog-api deploy v3.2.1
2026-03-12T22:28:01Z search-indexer reindex failed (nightly)Causality obvious in one column. Eyeballing three timezones hid it for 52 minutes.
We pinned the script output format in postmortem template — mandatory attachment before rollback approval.
Mental math vs script — why humans fail at PST+UTC+CET
I tried mental conversion at 2:45 PM. Got indexer spike 6 hours off.
PST to UTC in March: +7 or +8 depending on DST. CET to UTC: −1 or −2. I used +8 and −1. Indexer was on standard time edge case. Wrong.
$180/hour engineer doing timezone arithmetic during SEV2 is waste. Script does it once, correctly, in 200ms.
# normalize-log.sh excerpt
python3 -c "
import dateutil.parser
import sys
for line in sys.stdin:
ts = extract_timestamp(line)
print(dateutil.parser.parse(ts).astimezone(timezone.utc).isoformat(), line)
"Ugly Python. Cheaper than 52 minutes wrong rollback.
The catalog-api rollback collateral damage
catalog-api v3.2.1 contained legitimate pricing bug fix. Rollback reintroduced bug: 0.3% of SKUs showed wrong currency for 48 hours after Tuesday.
Separate incident ticket. Root cause of search latency: pool exhaustion. Root cause of currency bug return: bad timeline correlation.
Two incidents, one rollback. UTC timeline would have separated them at 2:31 PM.
OpenTelemetry migration — the permanent fix
6 weeks after Tuesday, all services export via OTel collector with enforced UTC timestamps at ingest.
# collector config
processors:
transform/logs:
log_statements:
- context: log
statements:
- set(time, Time(Now(), "UTC"))Legacy CET and PST configs overridden at collection. Humans can still misconfigure apps. Collector is backstop.
Correlation dashboard single timezone toggle for display. Storage UTC. On-call cannot disable.
The deploy annotation that saved March
March 18 — similar incident. Deploy at 14:29 UTC. Indexer errors at 06:28 UTC previous day.
Timeline script output made innocence obvious in 30 seconds. No rollback. Pool fix in 6 minutes.
Tuesday cost: 52 minutes + wrong rollback collateral. March cost: 6 minutes.
Same team. UTC enforcement between incidents. ROI on boring infrastructure.
Postmortem action items that stuck
→ All logs UTC at collector (done) → inc-timeline.sh in on-call toolkit (done) → Rollback requires timeline attachment (done) → Vendor logs excluded until TZ confirmed (done) → Quarterly timezone audit on 47 services (in progress)
Tuesday was expensive. March was cheap. Postmortem value measured in repeat incident cost, not document length.
On-call toolkit update — what’s in the bag now
→ inc-timeline.sh — normalize logs to UTC, sort, output → normalize-log.sh — vendor + app log converter → Grafana dashboard: "Deploy vs Errors UTC" → Runbook section: "Before rollback, attach timeline"
New on-call orientation: 30-minute timezone correlation drill. Fail the drill, shadow one more shift.
Boring onboarding. Cheaper than 52 minutes wrong service.
The rollback that should never have happened
catalog-api was innocent. Pool exhaustion on search-api was guilty. UTC timeline proves it in one screen.
We added rollback approval field: “deploy correlation evidence attached.” Tuesday would have stalled rollback pending timeline. Stall would have saved currency bug reintroduction.
Mechanical friction on rollback is feature when correlation is uncertain.

Froquiz’s Senior Dev scenarios include log correlation traps — practice spotting the timezone offset before you rollback the innocent deploy. → Froquiz
